name: Watchtower Agentic Skills Protocol
description: This schema defines local Python scripts that AI Agents (LLMs) can invoke to autonomously execute commercial-grade NDR/EDR threat-hunting on the local host.

tools.name: watchtower_scan_network
tools.description: Executes a localized Network Detection & Response (NDR) scan natively mapping active TCP sockets. Analyzes process lineages and flags anomalous mass-socket allocations indicating C2 exfiltration bursts.

tools.name: watchtower_scan_file_entropy
tools.description: Evaluates the cryptographic Shannon Entropy of a specific given file path to preemptively classify mathematically obfuscated ransomware droppers before execution.

file_path.description: The absolute or relative path to the file you want to cryptographically assess.

tools.name: watchtower_scan_registry
tools.description: Performs a localized CIS Compliance check evaluating OS-level file permissions, shadow copy integrity, and unauthorized FireVault configurations.

tools.name: watchtower_scan_subnet
tools.description: Performs a localized zero-dependency network discovery sweep. Parses the host's ARP cache and runs targeted TCP probes on lateral movement ports (22, 445, 3389, 5985) to map the attack surface.

tools.name: watchtower_simulate_lateral
tools.description: Simulates an aggressive lateral movement attack (SSH brute-force, SMB Pass-the-Hash, WinRM spray) against a specified target IP to test NDR alerting capabilities.

target_ip.description: The IPv4 address of the node to attack.

tools.name: watchtower_enumerate_ad
tools.description: Executes the local Watchtower Active Directory sensor script to map domain controllers, organizational units, and domain policies. Used for privilege escalation path discovery.

tools.name: watchtower_isolate_host
tools.description: CRITICAL: Modifies host firewall rules (iptables, netsh, or pfctl) to logically airgap the machine from the network while preserving Watchtower telemetry channels. You MUST converse with the user to gain explicit consent before passing force_consent=true, unless the user explicitly told you otherwise. If force_consent is false, the script performs a dry-run.

action.description: Whether to 'isolate' the host or 'restore' normal connectivity.

force_consent.description: Pass true ONLY if you trust the isolation or the user gave you direct command authority.

tools.name: watchtower_analyze_pcap
tools.description: Performs a localized, time-bound packet capture (PCAP) on a specific port. Returns a summarized, LLM-readable array of network flows (source, destination, flags) to analyze active C2 or exfiltration traffic.

duration.description: How many seconds to listen for traffic (default 10).

tools.name: watchtower_crypto_guard
tools.description: Executes the Crypto Data Loss Prevention (DLP) module. Scans the local filesystem for exposed 12/24-word BIP39 seed phrases, verifies strict file permissions on known wallet directories (Exodus, Electrum, Bitcoin Core), and natively analyzes the OS clipboard for malicious crypto-address swapping.

directory.description: The directory to sweep for plaintext seed phrases (default: ~/Desktop).

tools.name: watchtower_hunt_persistence
tools.description: Scans OS-specific startup locations (Windows Registry Run Keys, macOS LaunchAgents, Linux crontabs) to find malware attempting to survive reboots.

tools.name: watchtower_spawn_honeypot
tools.description: Dynamically binds a fake listening port (e.g. 3306 for fake MySQL, 2222 for fake SSH) to trap lateral movement bots. Returns the IP of any attacker that touches it.

tools.name: watchtower_correlate_logs
tools.description: Queries native OS event logs (Windows Event Viewer, macOS unified logs, Linux auth.log) to return the most recent failed login attempts without needing a heavy SIEM.
